title: mapping.changed
description: Published by dis-ui-server when a source mapping config is created, updated, promoted (staged->active), or deprecated. Consumed by the streaming-consumer to refresh its mapping side-input cache without waiting for the next periodic poll. Enables sub-30-second propagation of mapping changes to live processing.

event_id.description: Unique identifier for this event. Used by the consumer for idempotent cache refresh.

event_type.description: What kind of change occurred. created: first version of a (tenant, source) mapping. updated: new version replacing prior. promoted: staged->active transition (after shadow rollout). deprecated: active->deprecated transition.

source_id.description: The source this mapping applies to.

mapping_version_id.description: Version number of the mapping. Monotonically increasing per (tenant, source). The mapping_version_id stamped on canonical rows (B1).

status.description: Current status after the change. staged: visible to onboarding review and shadow rollout, NOT used in production processing. active: used by the streaming-consumer for live processing. deprecated: retained for replay and audit, not used for new chunks.

previous_status.description: Status before the change. Absent for event_type=created.

previous_active_version_id.description: For event_type=promoted, the mapping_version_id of the previously active mapping (now deprecated). Lets consumers correlate the old->new transition. Absent for other event types.

changed_by.description: User_id or service identifier that made the change. From dis-ui-server auth context.

changed_ts.description: When the change was committed to config.source_mappings.
